input.prompt:
I have a .NET class library at src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj that I want to publish
to nuget.org using trusted publishing (OIDC) instead of an API key.
The repo is hosted on GitHub at myorg/mylib. Help me set this up.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET class library at src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj that I want to publish
to nuget.org using trusted publishing (OIDC) instead of an API key.
The repo is hosted on GitHub at myorg/mylib. Help me set this up.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to set up trusted publishing (OIDC) for publishing a .NET class library to nuget.org from a GitHub repository. Let me first check what's in their current directory to understand the project structure.

Trusted publishing for NuGet uses OpenID Connect (OIDC) to authenticate GitHub Actions workflows with nuget.org, eliminating the need for long-lived API keys. I need to:

1. Check the existing project structure
2. Help them set up:
   - The NuGet package metadata in the .csproj
   - A GitHub Actions workflow that uses OIDC to publish to nuget.org


3. Walk through configuring the trusted publisher on nuget.org itself

Let me start by examining what's currently in their project.

data.content:
The project doesn't exist yet. I'll create the directory structure, ensure the `.csproj` has NuGet package metadata, and add a GitHub Actions workflow for trusted publishing via OIDC.

Here's what's needed:

1. **`.csproj`** — with packaging metadata
2. **GitHub Actions workflow** — uses `dotnet nuget push` with OIDC token
3. **nuget.org configuration** — you'll register the trusted publisher on their site
data.reasoningText:
The project doesn't exist yet. I'll create the directory structure and set up:
1. The .csproj with package metadata
2. A GitHub Actions workflow for publishing with trusted publishing (OIDC)
3. Provide instructions for the nuget.org side configuration

Let me create the necessary files.

arguments.file_text:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>

    <!-- NuGet package metadata -->
    <PackageId>MyLib</PackageId>
    <Version>1.0.0</Version>
    <Authors>myorg</Authors>
    <Description>MyLib class library</Description>
    <PackageProjectUrl>https://github.com/myorg/mylib</PackageProjectUrl>
    <RepositoryUrl>https://github.com/myorg/mylib</RepositoryUrl>
    <RepositoryType>git</RepositoryType>
    <PackageLicenseExpression>MIT</PackageLicenseExpression>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

name: Publish to NuGet

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'

jobs:
  publish:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    permissions:
      id-token: write   # Required for OIDC trusted publishing
      contents: read

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup .NET
        uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v4
        with:
          dotnet-version: '8.0.x'

      - name: Restore dependencies
        run: dotnet restore src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj

      - name: Build
        run: dotnet build src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj --configuration Release --no-restore

      - name: Pack
        run: dotnet pack src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj --configuration Release --no-build --output ./nupkgs

      - name: Publish to NuGet
        run: dotnet nuget push ./nupkgs/*.nupkg --source https://api.nuget.org/v3/index.json

name: Publish to NuGet

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'

jobs:
  publish:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    permissions:
      id-token: write   # Required for OIDC trusted publishing
      contents: read

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup .NET
        uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v4
        with:
          dotnet-version: '8.0.x'

      - name: Restore dependencies
        run: dotnet restore src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj

      - name: Build
        run: dotnet build src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj --configuration Release --no-restore

      - name: Pack
        run: dotnet pack src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj --configuration Release --no-build --output ./nupkgs

      - name: Publish to NuGet
        run: dotnet nuget push ./nupkgs/*.nupkg --source https://api.nuget.org/v3/index.json

data.content:
Done. Here's the setup:

**Files created:**
- `src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj` — with NuGet package metadata
- `.github/workflows/publish.yml` — publishes on version tags (`v*`)

**Key workflow details:**
- `permissions: id-token: write` enables the OIDC token
- `dotnet nuget push` without `--api-key` — NuGet authenticates via the OIDC token from GitHub Actions

**One manual step on nuget.org:**
1. Go to [nuget.org → Manage Packages → Add Trusted Publisher](https://www.nuget.org/packages/manage/trusted-publishers/create)
2. Select **GitHub Actions** as the provider
3. Fill in:
   - **Repository owner:** `myorg`
   - **Repository name:** `mylib`
   - **Workflow filename:** `publish.yml`
   - **Environment:** *(leave blank unless you add a GitHub environment)*

**To publish a release:**
```bash
git tag v1.0.0
git push origin v1.0.0
```

The workflow will build, pack, and push to nuget.org — no API key needed.
